primary_topics: Soil corrosion and underground storage tanks
Galvanic couples on plated stainless steel
cases_mentioned: U.S. Army underground storage tank study (1991, 15,000 tanks, CERL Urbana-Champaign)
West Virginia / Charleston water contamination (Elk River chemical spill, January 2014)
Copper-plated stainless steel tubing failure, 40-story Florida condo (1970s)
Gold-plated hydrocephalic shunt (Bob Rose anecdote, early 1970s)
New England Aquarium facade rusting from grinding marks
Stainless steel pacemaker battery cases with low-pH deionized rinse water
Quincy Shipyard barge stress corrosion cracking (brass heating pipes, pH 2.3 rinse)
Manwich (sloppy joe) processing plant 99-gallon tank failure (Knoxville, TN)
Admiralty brass heat exchanger silt pitting at utility
key_figures_and_references: 108 leaking tanks studied; quickest leaks in 6 years; 20 mils/year corrosion rate
100 ppm chloride water, 20-mil stainless perforated in 24 hours
Cr2(OH)3 passive layer ~50 nm thick on >10-12% Cr steel
ASME boiler and pressure vessel code 100-gallon exemption (drives 99-gallon vessel cluster)
Francis LaQue, *Marine Corrosion* (out of print), INCO
Kure Beach, NC and Severn River (Annapolis / Carderock) as canonical seawater corrosion test sites
